name: KOSPI (Korea Composite Stock Index)
summary: Created 2026-05-12. **A10 KOSPI 7,541.48 +0.6% MONDAY — RALLYING ON SAMSUNG HBM4 ANNOUNCEMENT + 11 'EMPEROR STOCKS' RECORD; BEHAVIOURAL DIVERGENCE FROM KINETIC-PREP TAPE.** Monthly +23.8%, 3M +32.8%. SK Hynix trades at ₩1.836M (+0.93%); 11 'emperor stocks' (>₩1M) hit record. Brokerages (Eugene Investment, Mirae Asset) maintained ₩3.2M SK Hynix target prices and ₩10,000 KOSPI outlook. **The Monday rally is the cleanest demonstration this cycle that the Friday -6.1% break was Samsung-strike-and-NYT-prep priced in at the close, not a leading indicator of further drawdown** — Friday's move was the panic, not the trend. **Helium clock Day 3 of 14 (TrendForce two-week count); no new allocation notice from Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC through Monday morning IST.** Kornbluth thesis remains operative but the KOSPI tape is now actively contradicting the panic-asymmetry case for the next 5 trading sessions. **Markets dossier proposes 'kospi-helium-decoupling' analytical edge weakening**: demote helium → kospi edge weight from current ~7-8 toward 5-6 if KOSPI rally extends through Tuesday-Wednesday. **Samsung 18-day strike still scheduled May 21**. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICE IS FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSE 7,493.18 (-6.1% 1D, +25.6% 1M, +32.0% 3M).** Intraday signature: KOSPI hit 8,046.78 (first-ever breach of 8,000) early session, then closed -488 pts. **Foreigners net sold ₩5.6128 trillion (largest of cycle); 7th consecutive session of foreign selling.** Per markets dossier C5: crisis vs non-crisis split ~30% crisis / ~70% Samsung-specific (the strike is a domestic operational shock, not crisis-linked). **B5 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE MAY 21 — 4 DAYS FROM START, NO NEW RESOLUTION DEVELOPMENT OVER THE WEEKEND.** Korea Herald / Tom's Hardware / Trading Key carried over from May 15-16 the unresolved union-management negotiation. Samsung labor union strike action remains scheduled May 21 to June 7 (18 days) with ~45,000 workers. KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric per yesterday's Kornbluth take. JPMorgan worst-case loss ₩43T anchor stands. **The Friday KOSPI -6.1% partial pre-validation of the trigger from yesterday's brief carries forward; the live test opens Wednesday May 20-21.** **Monday May 18 open is the first test of whether Friday's selloff was Samsung-strike priced-in or Iran-preparation-not-yet-priced-in** — the NYT preparation report adds a Korea-AI tail-risk overlay (helium clock Day 2 of 14 cumulates with Samsung strike). **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: Samsung component (~70%) — if union-management resolution before May 21, KOSPI bounces sharply; if strike begins, -10-15% further downside risk per JPMorgan ₩43T loss anchor. Crisis component (~30%) reverses on any genuine Iran-deal signal; further compounds if helium clock-expiry (Day 2 of 14) bites Q2. **PRIOR (May 16):** KOSPI -6.1% CONFIRMED CLOSE 7,493.18 (CNBC: 'South Korea's Kospi retreats from record high to drop over 6% as heavyweight tech names sell-off') — yesterday's intraday-roundtrip number now the official close.** SK Hynix capacity-zero framing and Samsung 18-day strike confirmation are the structural overhang for next week's open. **Markets dossier C3 triple-cause attribution**: (a) **Samsung 18-day strike CONFIRMED (~40%)** — NSEU confirmed May 21-June 7; JPMorgan ₩43T ($28.8B) worst-case loss; Samsung Electronics -8.6% Friday; Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI cap-weighted. (b) **Korea AI-Profit-Redistribution Proposal (~30%)** — Kim Yongbeom (Korean presidential office policy chief) May 11-12 Facebook proposal 'AI national dividend' using AI-boom-tax revenue; structural fear is windfall-tax precedent. (c) **Foreign Capital Outflow Cascade (~30%)** — foreign net sell ₩1.8T ($1.21B) on May 15 alone; 7th consecutive session; cumulative ₩5.6T over 7 sessions; USD/KRW ₩1,497.76 broke 1,500 intraday — FX-hedged foreign capital faces compounding losses. **NOT crisis-driven**: Iran rhetoric ~5%; Korea largely insulated from Brent direct; helium tail risk structural (~6-week buffer), not Friday-specific. **Crisis component: ~25%; 75% is domestic Korean dynamics.** **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: JPMorgan ₩43T worst-case structural; if strike resolves quickly OR Samsung resumes negotiations May 19-20, sharp bounce possible; full 18-day run = another -10-15% downside. **The 'Samsung 18-day strike → KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' trigger added yesterday is partially pre-validated by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI; the next 5 sessions are now the live test window.** PRIOR (May 15): **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 — THE SINGLE LARGEST EQUITY MOVE OF THE CYCLE**: KOSPI opened May 15 at 7,951, briefly broke 8,000 for the first time ever (intraday high 8,046.78), then reversed violently to close 7,493.20 — intraday roundtrip of ~7%, not a gap-down. **This is a forced unwinding off an extreme long, not a fundamental regime change.** **Primary chain (crisis-linked, ~2.4%)**: Trump told media overnight he 'would no longer tolerate' Iran + 'would no longer be patient,' floated entombing/removing Iran's enriched uranium stockpile (contradicting Trump-Xi joint statement diplomatic tone) → US after-hours futures rolled over → Seoul opened weak → helium overhang re-engaged (Korea sources 64.7% from Qatar; Samsung HeRS recovery and SK Hynix 6mo claim partially offsetting but May 16-23 window remains) → oil-import-bill pressure on USD/KRW (₩1,500.68, +0.7% — first breach of 1,500 psychological level) → foreign safe-haven USD bid. **Secondary chain (Samsung strike-specific, ~2.1%)**: Samsung union confirmed May 21-June 7 18-day strike (45,000+ workers) after talks broke down May 12-13; Industry Minister warned 100 trillion won damage; Samsung pre-emptively cut wafer input; Samsung Electronics intraday -5.4% from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won; sector-wide drag on KOSPI's largest weighting. **Tertiary chain (positioning, ~1.5%)**: Samsung + SK Hynix at 42.2% of KOSPI weight — record concentration; foreigners already net sold ₩27 trillion over prior 7 sessions; on May 15 alone foreigners dumped ₩4.4 trillion ($2.94B) by 2pm; +25.6% MoM rally was a coiled spring at 8,000 milestone. **ASSESSMENT: MOSTLY REVERSIBLE.** Samsung settles → ~35% recovers fast (May 13 precedent: Samsung recovered $66B intraday wipeout same-day on Seoul intervention). Iran de-escalates → ~40% recovers (helium overhang lifts, oil drops, won strengthens). Foreign positioning ~25% may not reverse quickly (concentration risk is structural). KB Securities raised 2026 target to 10,500 on May 14. **JPMorgan 12-month bull-case still 10,000 (current 7,493 = +33% upside if rally re-engages); Goldman Sachs reiterates 'top conviction' on Korean equities with 9,000 12m target.** **Helium 2-week-clock now at Day 74 since Ras Laffan halt; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — KOSPI most exposed major index; supply-side stack compounding (helium + 18-day Samsung strike May 21 + SK Hynix 'essentially zero' available capacity). **The +36.7% MoM rally hit Trump 'no longer patient' overnight + confirmed Samsung 18-day strike + foreign profit-taking + intraday Samsung Electronics -5.4% as it printed all-time high then reversed.** AI super-cycle dominant: **Samsung Electronics +111% YTD and SK Hynix +144% YTD = 42.2% of KOSPI**; Korea chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to record $8.54B/10 days. **JPMorgan raised KOSPI 12-month bull-case target to 10,000 (current ~7,942; +26% upside implied); Goldman Sachs reiterates 'top conviction' on Korean equities with 9,000 12m target.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) — won steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow. **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — KOSPI most exposed major index. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises** (-15-25% snap-back to gut KOSPI +36.7% MoM rally). Currently pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is most quantifiable real-world risk in entire complex. A13 HMM Namu situation unchanged — South Korean attribution 'various actors' hedge carries forward (no new amplification from US/Israeli press today). PRIOR (May 13): FM Cho Hyun's 'too early' on HMM Namu attribution + PPP opposition pressure DEFUSES immediate 'Korea forced to name Iran' diplomatic spiral. The 'double-tap drone' forensic confirmation is OPERATIONAL fact; the diplomatic implication is HEDGED. Korean helium-fab buffer ~4 weeks remains structural ceiling — Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' thesis intact one more session. KOSPI -1.7% May 12 selloff in yesterday's brief is now BETTER-UNDERSTOOD AS PARTIAL REPRICING OF DIPLOMATIC-FRICTION RISK rather than definitive Iran-attribution event.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D = DISCONNECT — currency says capital outflow, equity says relief). If both reverse together, regime change. **Asia split: KOSPI +2.8% relief vs SMH -2.6% / TAIEX -1.3% (semis cooled on AI-megacap profit-take, rotation into industrial-cyclicals Cu +2.5%).** Crisis-linked share: ~35% (relief), ~65% AI semis bid. Brent retrace + Trump-Xi summit-trade hopes = Asia ex-China-Japan reflation bid; KOSPI gets cleanest relief. **Watch USD/KRW: if both KOSPI bounce AND USD/KRW reverse together, regime change. Iran-named-attribution within 14 days would reprice helium tail risk in a hurry.** Reversibility: KOSPI bounce reverses on Iran-named-attribution; SMH unwind reverses on AI-capex catalyst return (NVIDIA earnings May 28). ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): KOSPI is primary Korean equity benchmark; persistent index for Korean memory-fab + AI-semis + helium cascade. **KOSPI 7,690.59 May 12 (-1.7% 1D — first profit-taking session of cycle; +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M / +78% YTD into May 11).** The largest 30-day move in any major-market index in recent memory. **Crisis-linked cascade path: war → Qatar Ras Laffan offline → helium constraint → 4-week countdown to Korean fab buffer exhaustion (mid-June) → Samsung/SK Hynix production-halt risk.** **B9 HMM Namu forensics public May 11: Korean joint investigation confirmed strike by 2 unidentified aerial objects ~1 min apart at stern; mine + torpedo RULED OUT; SEOUL STOPPED SHORT OF PUBLICLY BLAMING IRAN — creating diplomatic friction with Trump.** Korea has gone from 'passive helium victim' to 'active diplomatic actor.' USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) on Korea-Iran-attribution friction + capital outflow. Crisis-linked share: ~40% (helium tail + HMM Namu + diplomatic friction); secular ~60% (AI capex super-cycle dominating Samsung +111% YTD / SK Hynix +144% YTD). **STILL MOST REVERSIBLE POSITION IN WORLD if helium buffers deplete past mid-June.** Watch level: KOSPI 7,500 hold within 5 sessions confirms Korea AI-bid intact despite diplomatic friction.

current.samsung_sk_hynix_weight: 42.2% of index (Samsung +111% YTD, SK Hynix +144% YTD); chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to $8.54B/10 days
current.helium_clock_exposure: Most exposed major index to helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23; per markets dossier H reversibility: -15-25% snap-back if forced fab shutdown triggers

active_triggers.condition: KOSPI breaks below 7,500 sustained 3 sessions — confirms AI-bid cracking under diplomatic friction
Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage by mid-June
active_triggers.note: Inherits from helium node — most time-sensitive trigger in graph; ~4 weeks remaining.

recent_signals.headline: **KOSPI -3.3% TO 7,271.66 — IDIOSYNCRATIC, NOT IRAN-LINKED (~85% of move): SAMSUNG LABOR STRIKE RISK + 'AI PROFIT REDISTRIBUTION' POLICY + 8TH CONSECUTIVE DAY OF FOREIGN SELLING (>₩1T MAY 19).** Defense subsector ONLY green on the day. **CLEAN EXAMPLE OF A NON-CRISIS MARKET MOVE** — the investor should NOT read the KOSPI move as cascade-from-Iran. The helium-supply thesis (Kornbluth) is INDEPENDENT and structurally intact, but it is not what drove Tuesday's -3.3%. Background contributors (~15%): helium-clock Day 4 of 14 + Samsung strike date May 21 + Micron-style memory read-through. **Reversibility**: tomorrow's tape inverts if Samsung-labor settles; the 'AI profit redistribution' overhang persists until government clarifies. **For graph hygiene**: the helium → kospi edge should NOT be strengthened today (idiosyncratic dominant); the samsung-labor-strike-risk → kospi connection should be added as a new edge so the graph doesn't double-count it as crisis cascade.
recent_signals.verification: KOSPI -3.3% = CONFIRMED operational tape; Samsung labor strike risk = REPORTED; AI profit redistribution policy proposal = REPORTED; foreign selling 8th consecutive session = CONFIRMED; defense subsector only green = CONFIRMED (Seoul Economic Daily).

recent_signals.headline: **C4 KOSPI 7,941.95 (+1.2% 1D, +36.7% 1M, +39.9% 3M) — REBOUND CONTINUES** into Trump-Xi summit narrative. AI super-cycle dominant: Samsung +111% YTD / SK Hynix +144% YTD = 42.2% of KOSPI; chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to $8.54B/10 days. **JPMorgan raised KOSPI 12-month bull-case target to 10,000** (current +26% upside implied); **Goldman Sachs 'top conviction' with 9,000 12m target.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) won steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow. **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — KOSPI most exposed major index. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises** (-15-25% snap-back). Currently pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is most quantifiable real-world risk in entire complex. A13 HMM Namu situation unchanged (FM Cho 'various actors' hedge carries forward).
sources: CNBC May 12 (Samsung +111% YTD, SK Hynix +144% YTD = 42.2% KOSPI)
Korea Customs (chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to $8.54B/10 days)

'Double-tap drone' forensic confirmation is OPERATIONAL fact; diplomatic implication is HEDGED. Korean helium-fab buffer ~4 weeks remains structural ceiling. recent_signals.headline: KOSPI -1.7% 1D = FIRST PROFIT-TAKING SESSION OF CYCLE after extraordinary +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M / +78% YTD into May 11 — largest 30-day move in any major-market index in recent memory. Drivers: (1) HMM Namu forensics public May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran, creating diplomatic friction with Trump; (2) USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 +1.9% 1D on capital outflow; (3) mechanical profit-taking in a vertical market. Korea has gone from passive helium victim to active diplomatic actor. SMH $576.31 +1.7% 1D / +31.9% 1M; AI infrastructure CapEx demand from Microsoft/Meta/Amazon unbroken. Helium tail-risk timeline: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining to mid-June exhaustion. Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification still HOLDS but window contracting.
sources: Seoul official briefing + Bloomberg + Insurance Journal + Korea Herald (HMM Namu forensics)
